segments.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:
Can a man be of benefit to God? Can even a wise person benefit him?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: What pleasure would it give the Almighty if you were righteous? What would he gain if your ways were blameless?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Is it for your piety that he rebukes you and brings charges against you?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Is not your wickedness great? Are not your sins endless?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You demanded security from your relatives for no reason; you stripped people of their clothing, leaving them naked.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You gave no water to the weary and you withheld food from the hungry,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: though you were a powerful man, owning land— an honored man, living on it.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: And you sent widows away empty-handed and broke the strength of the fatherless.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: That is why snares are all around you, why sudden peril terrifies you,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: why it is so dark you cannot see, and why a flood of water covers you.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Is not God in the heights of heaven? And see how lofty are the highest stars!
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Yet you say, 'What does God know? Does he judge through such darkness?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Thick clouds veil him, so he does not see us as he goes about in the vaulted heavens.'
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Will you keep to the old path that the wicked have trod?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: They were carried off before their time, their foundations washed away by a flood.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: They said to God, 'Leave us alone! What can the Almighty do to us?'
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Yet it was he who filled their houses with good things, so I stand aloof from the plans of the wicked.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: The righteous see their ruin and rejoice; the innocent mock them, saying,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: 'Surely our foes are destroyed, and fire devours their wealth.'
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Submit to God and be at peace with him; in this way prosperity will come to you.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Accept instruction from his mouth and lay up his words in your heart.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: If you return to the Almighty, you will be restored: If you remove wickedness far from your tent
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: and assign your nuggets to the dust, your gold of Ophir to the rocks in the ravines,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: then the Almighty will be your gold, the choicest silver for you.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Surely then you will find delight in the Almighty and will lift up your face to God.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You will pray to him, and he will hear you, and you will fulfill your vows.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: What you decide on will be done, and light will shine on your ways.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: When people are brought low and you say, 'Lift them up!' then he will save the downcast.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: He will deliver even one who is not innocent, who will be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz
